title: Overall VB-MAPP Milestones Assessment Score
description: Evaluates the child's comprehension level and overall language acquisition to determine if they have the linguistic foundation to learn alongside other children in a less restrictive environment.
scoring_criteria: Level 1 scores: Needs an intensive and specialized intervention program (e.g., 1:1 ratio); peer interaction is secondary. Level 2 scores: Has basic skills and benefits from some group teaching and natural environment training, but still requires high-ratio intensive teaching for the bulk of instructional time. Level 3 scores: Has advanced mand, tact, and intraverbal skills, providing a linguistic foundation for advanced academic and social placement in a less restrictive setting.

title: Overall VB-MAPP Barriers Assessment Score
description: Identifies overall language and learning barriers that impede skill acquisition, determining the level of restriction needed for the child's daily intervention program.
scoring_criteria: High Score (31 to 96): Suggests the child is experiencing several barriers and needs a more restrictive program designed to focus on reducing or removing them. Lower Score (30 or less): Suggests a less restrictive placement may be successful depending on the specific barriers present.

title: VB-MAPP Barriers Assessment Score on Negative Behaviors and Instructional Control
description: Assesses severe behavior problems (screaming, aggression, non-compliance) and instructional control to determine if they limit access to less restrictive settings due to safety and teaching difficulties.
scoring_criteria: Score 1 or 2: Placement in a less restrictive classroom may be difficult, unsafe, and unproductive; requires a carefully designed behavior intervention program in a setting focused on behavior reduction. Score 3: Negative behaviors are less likely to regularly disrupt the educational process; more options available for placement. Score 4 or 5: Negative behaviors should not be an issue in deciding placement.

title: VB-MAPP Milestones Assessment Score on Classroom Routines and Group Skills
description: Evaluates the child's ability to follow classroom routines and learn effectively in a group-teaching format without requiring extensive prompting or a 1:1 ratio.
scoring_criteria: Score 1 or 2: Placement in a less restrictive classroom may be difficult and not productive; requires careful teaching procedures involving prompting, shaping, and rich schedules of reinforcement in a 1:1 or 1:2 ratio. Score 3: Intermediate group functioning. Score 4 or 5: The child follows classroom routines and learns in a group teaching format, marking a major component for progression to less restrictive settings.

title: VB-MAPP Milestones Assessment Score on Social Behavior and Social Play
description: Assesses the child's social skills, peer imitation, and play behavior to find an educational setting where their skills appropriately match the range of peers in the classroom, preventing social isolation.
scoring_criteria: Score 1 or 2 (with primarily Level 1 overall): Better served in a more restrictive setting with an intensive teaching format. Score 3 (with primarily Level 2 overall): Benefits from a less restrictive setting with partial integration into a classroom of typically developing peers (e.g., lunch, music, recess). Score 4 or 5 (with primarily Level 3 overall): Placement in a classroom with verbal and social peers would be highly beneficial; requires a less intensive teaching format.

title: Works Independently on Academic Tasks
description: Assesses a child's ability to independently engage in a specified activity or complete an assigned worksheet without teacher prompting to stay on task or stop disruptive behavior.
scoring_criteria: Score 1: Works independently for at least 30 seconds with only 1 adult prompt to stay on task. Score 4: Stays on task for 5 minutes without any adult prompts. Score 5: Demonstrates high independence suggesting the child will do well in a regular classroom with independent academic activities.

title: Generalization of Skills Across Time, Settings, Behaviors, Materials, and People
description: Assesses the child's ability to demonstrate stimulus and response generalization across different times, settings, people, and materials without the need for direct training for each variation.
scoring_criteria: Score 1: Only generalizes to other people, but not easily to other materials. Score 2-4: Gradual progression of generalization abilities. Score 5: Easily and frequently demonstrates both stimulus and response generalization.

title: Range of Items and Events that Function as Reinforcers
description: Assesses the variety of items and events that function as reinforcement, evaluating the transition from frequent, unlearned/innate reinforcers to less frequent, social, automatic, and age-appropriate reinforcers.
scoring_criteria: Score 1: Reinforcement must occur frequently and is mostly limited to unlearned (innate) reinforcers. Scores 2-4: Movement toward a wider variety of reinforcers that are social, age-appropriate, peer-delivered, change often, and are delivered less frequently. Score 5: Easily reinforced by social praise, verbal information, and age-appropriate games/toys/activities; reinforcers are widely varied and change frequently.

title: Rate of Acquisition of New Skills
description: Assesses the number of training trials required for the child to acquire a new skill (trials to criteria).
scoring_criteria: Score 1: Requires several days or weeks of training sessions that may contain hundreds of trials to acquire a new response, but eventually does so. Scores 2-4: Gradual progression toward fewer and fewer training trials necessary to acquire new skills. Score 5: Acquires new skills in only a few training trials.

title: Retention of New Skills
description: Assesses the child's ability to retain newly acquired skills over time without constant re-training or failure to maintain the skill.
scoring_criteria: Score 1: Successful with a short time delay (e.g., 20 minutes) after training. Scores 2-4: Gradual increase in time delay between training and testing, including maintenance trials (Score 4 specifically measures retention without maintenance trials). Score 5: Consistently maintains a new skill after long periods of time (e.g., 1 week) without maintenance trials.

title: 11. Learning from the Natural Environment
description: Assesses a child's ability to acquire new skills (motor, verbal, etc.) from natural consequences and observational learning in day-to-day activities without formal programming, structured teaching, or direct intervention.
scoring_criteria: 0: Does not meet criteria for 1. 1: Demonstrates at least two new skills that were acquired in the natural environment without the formal programming of antecedents and consequences. 2-4: Progression towards learning depending on the frequency and complexity of new skills acquired without formal or structured teaching. 5: Easily and frequently learns new skills in a natural environment setting.

title: 12. Demonstrates Transfer Between the Verbal Operants Without Training
description: Evaluates whether learning one type of verbal behavior automatically transfers to other types without direct training (e.g., learning a tact that naturally transfers to a mand without additional teaching trials).
scoring_criteria: 0: Does not meet criteria for 1. 1: Demonstrates transfer from one skill to another (e.g., echoic to mand) for two new verbal responses with two or less transfer training trials. 2-4: Progression towards more frequent transfers with less training and increasing complexity of verbal skills. 5: Transfer between the verbal skills occurs daily and reliably, and involves more advanced parts of speech (i.e., adjectives, prepositions, or adverbs) and intraverbal behavior.

title: 13. Adaptability to Change
description: Assesses the child's flexibility and ability to handle unexpected changes in routines, unplanned events, or disruptions without severe negative behavior, anxiety, or verbal perseveration.
scoring_criteria: 0: Does not meet criteria for 1. 1: Adapts to some changes with verbal preparation and pre-planning, but generally demonstrates negative behavior when unpredictable changes occur. 2-4: Progression towards improved adaptability to change in terms of a reduction of negative behavior and a reduction in the amount of preparation necessary to make changes. 5: Easily adapts to changes and disruptions in routines and activities and rarely emits any negative behavior when these changes occur.

title: 14. Spontaneous Behaviors
description: Evaluates the degree of spontaneity in a child's language and social skills, measuring if target behaviors are self-generated or if they are prompt-bound and rely on extraneous antecedent prompts.
scoring_criteria: 0: Does not meet criteria for 1. 1: Emits some behaviors without prompts, but most of his language and social interactions must be prompted. 2-4: Progression towards more spontaneous behavior as measured by less prompting, and a wider range of behaviors that occur spontaneously. 5: Emits strong and frequent spontaneous behaviors across all 16 areas of the VB-MAPP Milestones Assessment.

title: 15. Self-Directed Play and Leisure Skills
description: Evaluates a child's ability to independently entertain themselves and self-direct play and leisure time without adult prompting or reinforcement, especially during non-directed downtime in educational settings.
scoring_criteria: Score 0: Does not meet the criteria for a score of 1. Score 1: Total score on the VB-MAPP Milestones independent play area is at least 3. Scores 2-4: Progression toward the ability to independently engage in a variety of play and leisure activities without adult direction (e.g., playground play, toys, drawing, arts, sports, imaginary play). Score 5: Total score of at least 14 on the independent play area of the VB-MAPP Milestones Assessment.

title: 16. General Self-help Skills
description: Evaluates the child's ability to independently care for themselves based on motivation, self-initiation, attempting approximations, generalization, and the absence of negative behaviors during personal care routines (e.g., hand washing, dressing, brushing teeth).
scoring_criteria: Score 0: Does not meet the criteria for a score of 1. Score 1: No independent self-help, but usually does not engage in negative behaviors when adults provide personal care. Scores 2-4: Progression towards more independent and successful self-help (fading from physical to verbal prompts, more independent attempts, self-initiating, and partial success). Score 5: Motivated to perform tasks, initiates and attempts approximations, and generalizes skills across different settings and people.

title: 17. Toileting Skills
description: Evaluates the child's level of toileting skills, including toilet training readiness, bladder and bowel control, and independence in completing age-appropriate toileting routines.
scoring_criteria: Score 0: Does not meet the criteria for a score of 1. Score 1: Demonstrates toilet training readiness (e.g., dry periods, sits still for 2 minutes, shows indication of need) but still wears a diaper. Scores 2-4: Progression towards independent toileting, learning the toileting routines (undressing, wiping, flushing, washing), and the emergence of bladder and bowel control. Score 5: Initiates or mands to use the bathroom, and independently completes the age-appropriate steps of the toileting routine.

title: 18. Eating Skills
description: Evaluates the child's independent eating skills, allowing participation in lunch settings with typically developing peers with minimum supervision.
scoring_criteria: Score 0: Does not meet the criteria for a score of 1. Score 1: Can eat some things independently (highly desired finger foods) but usually requires full physical prompting to eat most things. Scores 2-4: Progression towards independent eating (requires fewer prompts, uses a spoon, independently gets/opens lunch box, cleans up). Score 5: Independently gets and opens own food items, eats without prompts, uses utensils, and cleans up with just a few prompts for the whole group.
